# Database Layer

> SQLite content store for blog posts and references. The dev server reads the prod DB over HTTPS; only the prod Docker container reads the file.

There is one source of truth: `/app/data/content.db` inside the prod Docker container on the VPS. `getDB()` in `src/lib/db.ts` returns `SqliteStore` when running in prod (`import.meta.env.PROD`) and `RemoteStore` (HTTPS client) everywhere else. There is no `BLOG_DB_MODE` toggle and no separate local DB the app reads. Dev requires `BLOG_API_KEY` in `.env`; `BLOG_REMOTE_URL` defaults to `https://samperalabs.com`.

A separate `scripts/manage-sqlite/content.db` file exists only as a working copy for ad-hoc CLI scripts (migrations, sitemap generation, batch retags). It is never read by the running app. Push/pull it via `scripts/manage-sqlite/index.py`.

## Schema

- [db/schema.sql](schema.sql): Posts and refs table definitions. `posts` has title, author, description, image_url/alt, pub_date, tags (JSON), content (Markdown), slug, status (`draft`/`published`, default `published`), published_at, deleted_at (soft-delete), timestamps. `refs` has title, description, format, tags (JSON), content, slug, timestamps. Status/published_at/deleted_at migrations on existing DBs run from `src/lib/db.ts` (`migratePostsAddStatusColumns`).

## Application Layer

- [src/lib/db.ts](../src/lib/db.ts): `PostStore` interface with two implementations — `SqliteStore` (better-sqlite3, used only when `import.meta.env.PROD`) and `RemoteStore` (HTTPS client against `/api/posts` using `BLOG_API_KEY`, used everywhere else). `getDB()` picks automatically. All methods Promise-returning.
- [db/README.md](README.md): Setup (dev + prod), schema, API endpoints, migration commands.

## Scripts

- [scripts/manage-sqlite/index.py](../scripts/manage-sqlite/index.py): SCP pull/push of the prod DB file to a local working copy at `scripts/manage-sqlite/content.db`. Used by ad-hoc migration scripts; not by the app.
